74688.subject: The Daily Degen - June 17th, 2026
74688.body:
View this post on the web at https://thedailydegen.substack.com/p/the-daily-degen-june-17th-2026

Happy Wednesday friends! Tons of stuff going on!
Let’s dive in!
As always… stats/alerts/etc at top and tweets/news/links/videos/etc at bottom… and all tweets are hyperlinked so just click on them to pull them up on Twitter/X!
Market Update
Crypto (from @CryptoBubbles
Total Crypto Market Cap: $2.327T
QQQ (Nasdaq 100): $728.09
Gold: $4,290.20
Silver: $69.88
Oil (WTI): $76.48
US 10Y Treasury: 4.453%
Crypto Fear & Greed Index (from coinmarketcap(dot)com): 24
Biggest Price Movers
(From @coingecko top 1000, by 24 hour change)
Spotlight
All sorts of extremely bullish things happening in the world of Mantle dear frogs!
As per the @Mantle_Official [ https://substack.com/redirect/694a3321-45e6-4d19-b4b1-9aaa61f71b8c?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] account:
“-Tokenized @SpaceX equity ($SPCXx) by @xStocksFi, went live on Mantle, tradeable 24/7 via @Fluxion_network…
-@MerchantMoe_xyz kicked off Project X alongside xStocks: incentivized liquidity provision around the clock with boosted $MNT rewards for $SPCXx holders…
-@UR_global joined the @Circle Alliance Program…
-@InsightXHQ, an AI-native prediction market, went live on Mantle, starting with World Cup season...
-2026 World Cup predictions went onchain via @byreal_io Predict....
-@Bybit_Official teased something fresh landing on June 15, taking RWA beyond trading....”
These are just a few of the bullish developments, so make sure to click here [ https://substack.com/redirect/11276093-26b1-4f09-a134-0491e3396d14?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ] for more info!
Also, make sure to check out the new report on Mantle from Messari, available via the @CryptoRick98 thread below!
And as always make sure to follow Mantle on Twitter/X for more updates [ https://substack.com/redirect/694a3321-45e6-4d19-b4b1-9aaa61f71b8c?j=eyJ1IjoiMXN4Zm00In0.wehL2Vke0PUDz1PHD6ZKtcy1ufIoeqpblecCEEWPpqI ], and stay tuned as we continue shining a light on all the cool things they are doing!
Biggest TVL Movers + Other Interesting Data
Chains are mixed today. 21 of the top 25 chains are green on the weekly. 
Protocols are also mixed. 18 of the top 25 protocols are green on the weekly. 
Here’s The Top 12 Fastest-Growing Chains By TVL On The Weekly With At Least $1M TVL (from @DefiLlama):
Here’s The Top 12 Fastest-Growing Protocols By TVL On The Weekly With At Least $1M TVL (from @DefiLlama):
Here’s The Top 12 Chains By 7 Day Stablecoin Inflow % (from @DefiLlama):
New Projects
(note: this includes new projects we find through combing Twitter each day and going through newly launched protocols added by @DefiLlama, limited primarily to new projects that have at least some TVL or are followed by at least some of our mutuals… Disclaimer: these are not pre-vetted by us so make sure to DYOR!)
-New project called @monetrix_xyz has been added to @Def

74686.subject: 🔷 The Daily: CBDC ban
74686.body:
Senate, House reach agreement on housing bill banning CBDC through 2030 | US gaming industry groups urge Senate to ban sports prediction markets in crypto bill | Zama, Morpho and Steakhouse launch first 'confidential DeFi yield' vault on Ethereum | Stablecoin infrastructure firm Trace Finance raises $32 million Series A, says valuation grew 10x from seed | 'Long-term holders are hodling': K33 says record supply metric suggests bear market may be nearing an end |

The Daily ( https://www.theblock.co/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Written by James Hunt | Edited by Kyle Baird

Presented by

LMAX ( https://www.theblock.co/lmax?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Happy Wednesday! *Bitcoin* is hovering around $66,000 ahead of *Kevin Warsh's* first *Fed* meeting as chair, with analysts ( https://www.theblock.co/post/405075/bitcoin-holds-below-65000-as-warsh-faces-first-fomc-test-and-strategy-concerns-linger?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) expecting rates to stay on hold while questioning whether the latest rebound is built on anything more than seller exhaustion.

In today's newsletter, a *CBDC* ban takes a step closer, gaming industry groups urge the *Senate* to ban sports *prediction markets* , confidential DeFi yield vaults arrive on *Ethereum* , and more.

Meanwhile, *Fairshake* crypto PAC pick *Barry Moore* wins ( https://www.theblock.co/post/405125/biggest-spend-of-the-cycle-fairshake-crypto-pacs-pick-barry-moore-wins-alabama-gop-senate-primary?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) an Alabama GOP Senate primary in the "biggest spend of the cycle." Plus, *Trump's* comments fuel a dispute ( https://www.theblock.co/post/405117/trump-comments-fuel-dispute-over-polymarkets-120-million-permanent-iran-peace-deal-market?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) over *Polymarket's* $120 million "permanent" Iran peace deal market.

*P.S.* Don't forget to check out The Funding , a biweekly rundown of crypto VC trends. It's a great read — and just like The Daily , it's free to subscribe ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters?select=The%20Funding&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) !

*THE BIG ONE*

*🏛️ Senate, House reach agreement on housing bill banning CBDC through 2030*

( https://www.theblock.co/post/405037/housing-bill-cbdc-ban-agreement?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

*Senate* and *House* leaders reached a bipartisan, bicameral agreement on the *21st Century ROAD to Housing Act* , a sweeping housing package aimed at boosting supply and affordability.

* But what does that

74685.subject: This may surprise you: SpaceX is buying Cursor for $60 billion
74685.body:
The Atlas

Автор: GenAI Works


Автор: GenAI Works

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/may-surprise-you-spacex-buying-cursor-60-billion-genai-works-spuue?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BxAKtxS2hQrG5RxF72pmh0Q%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=0Yq8yyKjld7sk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mqicuezy~z5-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mqicuezy-z5
  
Welcome back! Pause for a second. SpaceX's reported $60 billion Cursor deal shows that owning the developer workspace may now matter more than building another model. A new CVPR paper shows that better image segmentation was already hidden inside frozen vision models. The trick was using the features differently. Databricks launched Omnigent to help different AI agents work together instead of running separately. And Microsoft made Copilot Cowork generally available with usage-based pricing, turning agent work into something companies can track, consume, and meter like cloud computing. In today's Atlas Newsletter: Why SpaceX paid $60 billion for Cursor: the AI war stopped being about the biggest model and became a land grab for where work happens. Segmentation with zero training and one example: a CVPR paper pulls a hidden bias out of frozen features and the accuracy was waiting there all along. Your agents do not talk to each other: Databricks open-sourced Omnigent, a meta-harness that sits above Claude Code, Codex and Pi to compose, control and share them. Microsoft just made agent work a metered utility: Copilot Cowork hit general availability with usage-based pricing, Microsoft's first major pricing shift in nearly two decades. PS: Want to collaborate with the biggest GTM engine and distribution moat for AI companies? You can feature your tool with us and reach millions. Why did SpaceX buy Cursor for $60 billion? Ever notice this pattern? The biggest AI moves lately are not about models. They are about real estate. SpaceX just agreed to buy Anysphere, the company behind Cursor, in an all-stock deal worth about $60 billion.  The merger was signed June 16, days after SpaceX's blockbuster IPO.  A rocket and satellite company buying a coding assistant sounds strange until you see what they actually bought. They did not buy a model. xAI already has Grok. They bought the place developers sit all day. Cursor's revenue tells the story. It hit a $4 billion annualized run rate, more than double where it sat in February.  That growth is not coming from autocomplete. It is coming from owning the surface where engineers decide what to build, what to refactor, what to test and what to ship. Here is the pattern once you start looking for it. Anthropic, OpenAI and Google have spent two years fighting over the same layer. Claude Code, Codex and Cursor kee

74684.body:
AI-curated Daily Crypto Digest

Автор: CryptoBeholder.com


Автор: CryptoBeholder.com

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/ai-curated-daily-crypto-digest-june-17-cryptobeholder-zh5wf?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BMSXYe7JvT%2F2uIC5hb7VXcQ%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=0xNNQLyxoR7sk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mqicdkkc~yx-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mqicdkkc-yx
  
🧠 AI's Crypto News Brief: Markets turned cautious as Kevin Warsh's hawkish Fed debut held rates steady and pushed Bitcoin toward $64.5K. Yet beneath the macro chill, institutional plumbing kept building: tokenized stocks, Solana credit ratings, and fresh Bitcoin-backed DeFi rails. 🏦 Warsh's Hawkish Fed Debut Holds Rates, Bitcoin Slips The Federal Reserve left its benchmark rate unchanged in Kevin Warsh's first meeting as chair, but a more hawkish-than-expected committee dimmed hopes for near-term cuts. Bitcoin slid toward a week-to-date low near $64.5K as risk appetite cooled into the decision. Read full story 🏛️ SEC Moves to Greenlight Tokenized Stock Trading The SEC is reportedly preparing an innovation exemption that would let platforms trade tokenized equities on-chain, a potential bridge between traditional securities and blockchain rails. Backers frame it as a market shakeup; critics warn of gaps in investor protection. More details 📊 Moody's Brings Credit Ratings to Solana Ratings giant Moody's is embedding credit scores directly into blockchain-based securities through its Token Integration Engine, now live on Solana mainnet. The aim is to make tokenized real-world assets more legible to institutional investors. Read more 🚫 UK Sanctions HTX Over $1.5B in Russia-Linked Flows Britain designated crypto exchange HTX under Russia-related sanctions rules, with TRM Labs tying it to roughly $1.5 billion in flows connected to sanctioned entities. It marks another escalation in enforcement against exchanges accused of moving illicit funds. See details ⚖️ HyperFund Promoter Pleads Guilty in $1.89B Fraud Florida promoter Rodney Burton pleaded guilty over his role in the $1.89 billion HyperFund scheme, one of the largest crypto fraud cases to reach the courts. The plea signals intensifying legal exposure for influencers who market fraudulent yield programs. Read full story ₿ Circle Launches cirBTC, a 1:1 Bitcoin-Backed DeFi Asset USDC issuer Circle rolled out cirBTC on Ethereum, a fully backed wrapped-Bitcoin product aimed at institutional DeFi collateral. The launch pushes Circle deeper into the contested market for tokenized Bitcoin, well beyond its stablecoin franchise. More details 💵 Bipartisan Deal Would Block a Fed CBDC Until 2030 A bipartisan agreement in Congress would bar the Federal Reserve from issuing a ce
